title: Airbnb Keeps Generating Strong FCF and FCF Margins and Could Be 15%-20% Too Cheap
text: Airbnb's trailing twelve-month (TTM) free cash flow was $4.5 billion, representing a TTM FCF margin of 38%. Full year 2025 revenue was $12.2 billion with approximately $4.6 billion of free cash flow, a roughly 38% FCF margin that puts ABNB among the best cash generators in consumer internet. Airbnb's Q3 results showed +9.7% year-over-year revenue growth and 11.5% free cash flow growth, with an FCF margin that was strong at over 38% on a trailing 12-month basis. Adjusted EBITDA was over $2.0 billion in Q3 2025, the company's highest in any quarter. The company runs a roughly 35% adjusted EBITDA margin while still investing heavily in product. Revenue increased 10 percent year-over-year in Q3 2025, landing at the high end of the company's guidance range. Gross Booking Value increased 14 percent year-over-year, while Nights and Seats Booked rose by nine percent, with both metrics accelerating from Q2 and exceeding expectations. The balance sheet holds $10.6 billion in liquidity against just $2 billion in debt, and a $6 billion buyback authorization signals management confidence. Overall, Airbnb demonstrates strong profitability with industry-leading cash flow generation, representing a robust financial performance for the company.
